# codex-swarm v0.7.1

This hotfix corrects the Windows process-handle boundary for asynchronous
app-server spawning introduced in v0.7.0.

## Fixed

- Detached caller-owned `csd appserver-runtime` processes no longer inherit
  the spawning CLI's stderr pipe. PowerShell and other pipe-aware callers now
  receive the durable worker, thread, and turn identity as soon as it is
  recorded while the first agent turn continues asynchronously.

## Verified

- The exact interactive acceptance smoke returned a durable worker and thread
  in 18.209 seconds while its instructed 20-second agent turn was still
  running, then the worker completed with `ASYNC-SPAWN-SMOKE`.
- The standard Go formatting, vet, test, build, race, vulnerability, and action
  lint gates pass for the release commit.
